next_head_excerpt:
# Chapter 18

The shelf bought them one breath.

Kem hit it first, both hands out, boots skidding on the polished stone. Vel came in after him hard enough to jar the old crack in her left ribs. The rock covered them from the waist down and almost nowhere that mattered above it.

Across the basin, Commander Osen did not raise her voice.

"You can't make it work," she called. "It's not enough anymore."

Vel kept her head low and looked anyway. The floor of the Scour spread west in red-gray shelves and wind-cut troughs, eight hundred meters of bad stone and no mercy in the layout. The threshold point was still a pale, unmarked patch near Osen's boots. Two descent agents were already fanning wide from the switchback. The circuiting specialist was cutting south to turn the shelf into a pocket. Farther east, two basin garrison fighters were moving for angles. Nobody was rushing. That was the part that made it hard.

Osen knew exactly how much time sat in eight and twelve.

The first Combat Pulse hit the top lip of the shelf and took a slab of sandstone off with it. Chips sprayed down the back of Vel's neck. Kem folded tighter against the rock on instinct.

"Was that supposed to be a warning?" he asked.

"No."

Another Pulse shaved the left side. Osen was not trying to dig them out by force. She was cutting the cover into smaller pieces and letting the basin do the rest.

Vel tracked the angles in her head and did the arithmetic she had been doing since the tunnel mouth. Twenty levels between them had looked workable in the dark, in the journal, in every stupid year she had built...
open_hooks_to_carry: The Scour as landscape: vast erosion basin, exposed rock, hostile terrain
Red Reserve permanent garrison: a commander who has guarded the site for five years, witnessed three crossings, knows what zero is — plus two freshly deployed agents for the Kem retrieval; four total

The late complication: the commander reveals the threshold has degraded — natural erosion compounded by three years of deliberate dampening; a two-person cascade may not be enough; Vel's eight-year-old calculations are outdated
Vel at 16 and Kem at 18: the numbers against them rendered in dark comedy — 'I'm finally discreet enough for the Conservatory Rite'
The tunnel approach: Kem spends 6 levels (light, reinforcement, defensive), Vel spends 6 (Perception mapping, tunnel navigation, evasion); Vel at 8, Kem at 12
The commander as mirror-Sable: competent, spent, loyal to a flawed system, guarding something she knows is real because the alternative would collapse everything
Intercut: Sable trailing three hours behind at 236, spending on tracking, making incremental decisions toward the Scour

payoffs_to_land.description: The permanent Scour garrison is confirmed — the commander has been here for years, knows the threshold intimately, and has standing orders to prevent crossings

prev_tail_excerpt:
...would have spent in the Margins."

"I know."

"I'm saying it because I don't want memory cleaning it up later."

"I'd have done it anyway." He shut the eye again. "Even if you'd told me clean. Maybe especially then. I don't know what that says about me."

"That you're not Hoarded enough to watch a child drown and call it prudence."

"That sounded almost kind."

"Don't get used to it."

"Too late."

They let the night thicken around that. No fire. No talking once full dark settled. Once, far to the south, a brief pale flash climbed the rim line and vanished. Signal plate or lantern shutter or moon on metal. Vel could not tell. She did not like not being able to tell.

Fourteen hummed at the edge of her awareness. Eighteen hummed at his. Thirty-two lay between them and a permanent garrison at the Scour. Dren was somewhere north with a ruined arm, one hundred and twenty levels, and an eight-year-old girl waiting for a healer to make her legs hold. Vel kept seeing his face when he'd said *I've been picking her up off the kitchen floor since spring.* She kept seeing Maren in the falling room. She kept seeing Kem in the hollow under the wall, finally asking the question with no place left to set it down unfinished.

Sometime after midnight Kem spoke into the dark without opening his eyes.

"No more surprises," he said.

Vel listened to the wind for a beat before answering.

"I'll do what I can," she said.

It wasn't a promise. He knew that. So did she. But in the room with the blank liability form on the wall and the journal between them, it was the straightest line she had left.

setups_to_plant.description: The threshold has been deliberately weakened — a two-person cascade may not generate enough energy. This creates a structural necessity for a third contributor, making Sable's arrival essential rather than convenient.

state_deltas_required: Near-zero levels vs. dangerous terrain and trained Red Reserve garrison; the late complication that the threshold has degraded

Every remaining ability becomes life-or-death; the late complication reveals the threshold has degraded and two spenders may not be enough; the Hoarded's active suppression is confirmed; Sable's approach seeded through incremental decisions
